'''Adam Cook''' (born 14 November 2000) is an [[Australia]]n professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Charters Towers, Queensland]], Cook played his junior rugby league for Townsville Brothers and attended [[Ignatius Park College]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/players/adam-cook/|title=Adam Cook|website=18th Man}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2017/08/04/nq-spirit-alive-for-age-group-finals/|title=NQ spirit alive for age-group finals|date=4 August 2017|publisher=NQ Cowboys}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/ignatius-park-go-down-fighting-in-confraternity-final/news-story/4e6a14990f551bd347b2c36a9133321e|title=Ignatius Park go down fighting in Confraternity final|date=1 July 2017|website=Townsville Bulletin}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2016, Cook played for the [[Townsville Blackhawks]] [[Cyril Connell Cup]] side, starting at halfback in their Grand Final win over the [[Souths Logan Magpies]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/05/08/blackhawks-win-connell-cup-in-thriller/|title=Blackhawks win Connell Cup in thriller|date=8 May 2016|website=QRL}}</ref> In 2017, he moved up to the club's [[Mal Meninga Cup]] side.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-mm-cup-teams-2017-round-1/|title=QRL Mal Meninga Cup - 2017 Round 1 TEAMS|date=4 March 2017|website=League Unlimited}}</ref> In 2018, after starting the season in the Mal Meninga Cup, Cook moved up to the Blackhawks' [[Hastings Deering Colts]] side, starting at halfback in their Grand Final loss to the [[Norths Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2018/09/25/knights-recruit-crowned-colts-man-of-the-match/|title=Knights recruit crowned Colts man of the match|date=25 September 2018|publisher=Newcastle Knights}}</ref> In 2019, Cook joined to the Blackhawks' [[Queensland Cup]] squad, but did not play a game, spending the entire season with the Colts team, where he was named the club's under-20 Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://blackhawkstsv.com.au/2018/11/22/pathway-leads-to-locals-success/|title=Pathway Leads to Local Success|date=22 November 2018|publisher=Towsnville Blackhawks}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://blackhawkstsv.com.au/2019/09/13/2019-mendi-blackhawks-presentation-night-award-winners/|title=Blackhawks presentation night award winners|date=13 September 2019|publisher=Townsville Blackhawks}}</ref>

In October 2019, Cook signed with the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/cowboys-lose-out-at-kirwan-high-leaders-sign-contracts-with-nrl-rivals/news-story/cf579cde6b20dd0d0f534f7c638893a2|title=Cowboys lose out at Kirwan High leaders sign contracts with NRL rivals
|date=16 October 2019|website=Townsville Bulletin}}</ref>

===2020===
In round 20 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Cook made his NRL debut for Canberra against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2020/09/22/nrl-team-list-raiders-v-sharks/|title=NRL Team List: Raiders v Sharks|date=22 September 2020|publisher=Canberra Raiders}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-09-26/nrl-parramatta-beats-wests-tigers-as-panthers-raiders-post-wins/12707470/|title=Parramatta beats Wests Tigers to claim third spot on NRL ladder, Panthers and Raiders score wins|website=abc.net.au}}</ref>

===2021 & 2022===
Between 2021 and 2022, Cook played for [[Redcliffe Dolphins|Redcliffe]] and [[Mackay Cutters|Mackay]] in the Queensland Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/players/qrl-premiership/redcliffe-dolphins/adam-cook/|title=Adam Cook Queensland Cup profile|website=qrl.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
In 2023, Cook was part of [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]]'s top 30 squad but continued to play for Mackay in the Queensland Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mackayandwhitsundaylife.com/article/cutters-shine-in-cowboys-trial/|title=Cutters Shine In Cowboys Trial|website=Mackay and Whit Sunday Life}}</ref> Cook had resigned with the Canberra Raiders for the 2024 season, and made his first appearance in the Pre Season Challenge, against the Parramatta Eels, scoring a try in an impressive win by the Capital club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/02/17/raiders-roll-eels-with-big-second-half/ |title=Raiders roll Eels with big second half |website=NRL |date=17 February 2024 |access-date=18 February 2024}}</ref>

===2024===
Cook played a total of six matches for Canberra in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-canberra-raiders-analysis-exclusive-20240911-p5k9ws.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: The 'wasted' enforcer Ricky Stuart must 'fire up' in 2025|website=nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Cook was released by Canberra and signed a three-year deal with the Leigh Leopards in the Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-10-23 |title=Adam Cook: Leigh Leopards sign half-back from NRL side Canberra Raiders |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce864298902o |access-date=2025-10-24 |publisher=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref>
